article.body_preview:
DHAKA, June 7, 2023 (BSS) - Federation of Bangladesh Chambers of Commerce and Industry (FBCCI) President Md Jashim Uddin today urged the government to extend policy support towards import-substitute industries in the country to lessen import pressure on foreign exchange.

The FBCCI president made the call while addressing a post-budget discussion as a special guest organized by the Economic Reporters' Forum (ERF) jointly with RAPID and Asia Foundation held at the ERF auditorium at Paltan in the

llm_connections.evidence_quote: urged the government to extend policy support towards import-substitute industries in the country to lessen import pressure on foreign exchange.
llm_connections.scope_justification: The statement explicitly links national-level policy support for import-substitute industries to lessening import pressure, which concerns country-wide imports that form the imports component of the balance of trade.

llm_connections.evidence_quote: the Bangladesh Bank's move only to cut imports is not a solution to reduce stress on the dollar
llm_connections.scope_justification: The quote explicitly discusses 'stress on the dollar' (the USD/BDT exchange rate) and links it causally to import-cutting measures, addressing exchange-rate conditions within the currency_log_return scope.
